commit.message:
fix: boolean false overrides dropped in multi-document helmfiles (#2527) (#2532)

* fix: environment values pollution causing boolean false overrides to be dropped (#2527)

PR #2367 introduced envCopy.Values = values in NewEnvironmentTemplateData,
where values = GetMergedValues() = Defaults + Values + CLIOverrides. This
caused .Environment.Values to include Defaults, so when multi-part helmfiles
re-assigned environment values via {{ toYaml .Environment.Values }}, Defaults
values (e.g. helmDefaults.atomic: true) were written into the environment
Values field. Later, GetMergedValues() applied Values over Defaults, causing
the stale atomic: true to win over the correct atomic: false override.

Fix: set .Environment.Values to Values + CLIOverrides only (excluding Defaults),
so re-assignment patterns don't pollute the Values layer with Defaults.

Signed-off-by: yxx <yxx@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: yxxhero <aiopsclub@163.com>

* fix: rename test to correctly reflect Values override Defaults precedence

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/helmfile/helmfile/sessions/1b251877-7050-404b-8cc7-abd6aa3ec36b

Co-authored-by: yxxhero <11087727+yxxhero@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: flip regression test fixture to exercise false override (issue #2527)

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/helmfile/helmfile/sessions/c428fd46-b698-4e88-bff2-4c9ac72d2deb

Co-authored-by: yxxhero <11087727+yxxhero@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: yxx <yxx@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
test: add 95 learning system integration tests

- Mastery algorithm: Unknown→Learning→Known lifecycle (14 tests)
- Spaced repetition: SM-2 intervals, EaseFactor, due filtering (13 tests)
- Multi-day journey: 5-day learner simulation with plan coherence (7 tests)
- Plan→progress lifecycle: full plan-practice-record cycle (9 tests)
- Progress caching: cache types, user isolation, invalidation (26 tests)
- Edge cases: IsKnown boundaries, EffectiveStreak, grace period (26 tests)

364/364 tests pass (95 new + 269 existing).

feat(zsh): add git wip workflow commands

add:
- shared Git repo, branch, and worktree validation helpers in `files/zsh/lib/git.zsh`
- `Update-GitStableBranch` to switch to `stable` and fast-forward from `origin/stable`
- `Update-GitWIPBranchHistory` to rebase the current `wip/*` branch onto `stable`
- `Complete-GitWIPBranch` to fast-forward `stable` to the current `wip/*` branch
- `Remove-GitWIPBranch` to delete merged local `wip/*` branches safely
- lowercase aliases for the Git WIP workflow commands

modify:
- `New-GitWIPBranch` to require a clean repo and start only from `stable`
- `docs/git.md` to document the `stable -> wip/* -> stable` workflow

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add Guice dependency to build.sbt

Enable Guice DI for the Play Java project by adding `libraryDependencies += guice` to build.sbt. The change currently adds the dependency twice; remove the duplicate line to avoid redundancy.

* Add 'guice' to cspell words

Include "guice" in cspell.json's words array to prevent the spellchecker from marking the Guice library name as a misspelling. This updates the project's cspell configuration.

commit.message:
docs: simplify README - clear narrative flow, collapsible quickstarts, capability table (#935)

Restructure README from 430-line wall-of-text to scannable narrative:

- Add 'What This Is (and Isn't)' positioning section up front
- Add 'How it works' one-liner flow diagram
- Add 'Why it matters' with benchmark citation
- Consolidate 5 language quickstarts into primary + collapsible details
- Replace bullet-point highlights with 'What You Get' capability table

feat: send wipe_assets to device on adoption and deletion (#84) (#165)

When a device is adopted or deleted from the CMS, send a wipe_assets
WebSocket command so it clears cached videos, images, and schedule
state. This prevents stale content from a previous adoption lingering
on re-adopted or deleted devices.

- Add WIPE_ASSETS / WIPE_ASSETS_ACK to the WS protocol
- adopt_device: send wipe_assets(reason=adopted) before sync
- delete_device: send wipe_assets(reason=deleted) before DB removal
- Handle wipe_assets_ack in WS message loop
- Update OpenAPI descriptions
- Add 5 tests (online adopt, orphan adopt, delete, offline adopt/delete)
- Bump version 1.14.0 → 1.15.0

commit.message:
Fix set 4 suggestion: go heavier than last set, not capped at historical max

The set 4 prescription was being clamped to max_entered_weight (historical max),
which made it identical to set 3. Set 4 exists specifically to push past the
plateau — ensure it's at least 10% heavier than the last set, and allow up to
25% above historical max.

commit.message:
docs: add design spec for version and about menu fixes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Castellarius: comprehensive state machine simplification (#422)

## Summary

Accumulated hardening fixes had turned the Castellarius into a
non-deterministic mess that could burn LLM tokens in respawn loops and
other edge cases. This PR strips all of that back to the clean
invariants.

## Invariants restored

1. Only agents write outcomes - Castellarius never calls SetOutcome
2. Castellarius never kills sessions - if tmux is alive, let it run
3. Every spawn is always fresh - no --continue/resume logic
4. Sessions exit automatically - exec prefix + remain-on-exit off make
zombie state structurally impossible

## Changes

### Deleted entirely
- dispatch_loop.go + tests - race-prone heuristic recovery loop
- stuck_delivery.go + tests - invasive session killing mechanism

### scheduler.go
- Removed phantom commit check (false positives)
- Removed auto-promote (masked misbehaviour)
- Removed dirty worktree redundant checks
- Removed dirtyNonContextFiles(), sandboxHead(), GetLastReviewedCommit
- Removed dispatchLoop, rebaseAndPushFn, ghMergeFn, findPRFn,
killSessionFn struct fields

### session.go (cataractae)
- Removed priorSessionCount(), buildContinueCmd(), --continue flag -
always fresh spawn
- Prepend exec to agent command: shell replaced by agent, pane closes
when agent exits
- Set remain-on-exit off after session creation to override any user
tmux.conf
- Deleted isAgentAlive() and tmuxDisplayMessage - zombie state is now
structurally impossible

### Tests
- Fixed 2 flaky timing tests using waitCalls instead of time.Sleep(50ms)
- Updated all HasPrefix assertions for exec prefix
- Deleted TestIsAgentAlive_* tests (5 tests)

---------
